1	# Design Document — TASK-121 ChargeBay Offline Operations Console
2	
3	## 1. Project Overview
4	A fully offline, browser-based React SPA for EV charging site teams. All data lives in IndexedDB (via Dexie.js) with hot/cold tiering. No backend, no network calls. Designed for single-device kiosk deployment.
5	
6	## 2. Tech Stack
7	
8	| Layer | Library | Version | Purpose |
9	|---|---|---|---|
10	| Framework | React | 18.x | Component model |
11	| Routing | React Router | 6.x | Kiosk + admin SPA navigation |
12	| Local DB | Dexie.js | 3.x | IndexedDB wrapper (hot + cold stores) |
13	| QR Code | qrcode | 1.x | Local QR code generation |
14	| Excel Parse | xlsx (SheetJS) | 0.18.x | .xlsx bulk import |
15	| Crypto | WebCrypto (built-in) | — | PBKDF2, AES-GCM |
16	| Testing | Vitest | 1.x | Unit & component tests |
17	| Testing | React Testing Library | 14.x | Component interaction tests |
18	| Testing | Playwright | 1.x | E2E smoke tests |
19	| Build | Vite | 5.x | Dev server + bundle |
20	| Language | TypeScript | 5.x | Type safety |
21	
22	## 3. Architecture
23	
24	### 3.1 Navigation Modes
25	```
26	/login
27	  → Attendant / Site Manager → /kiosk  (KioskShell: full-screen, tab-bar)
28	  → Administrator / Auditor  → /admin  (AdminShell: sidebar)
29	```
30	
31	### 3.2 Service Layer Pattern
32	```
33	UI (Pages + Components)
34	        ↓
35	  React Hooks / Context (useAuth, useNotifications)
36	        ↓
37	  Service Layer (pure async TS — no React imports)
38	        ↓
39	  Dexie DB Layer (hot store + cold store)
40	```
41	
42	Services accept `actor: User` for RBAC and audit logging. Independently unit-testable.
43	
44	### 3.3 RBAC Matrix
45	
46	| Feature | SysAdmin | SiteManager | Attendant | Auditor |
47	|---|---|---|---|---|
48	| Create/manage reservations | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — |
49	| Check in (confirm arrival) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — |
50	| Complete sessions | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — |
51	| Approve compensation | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
52	| Refund orders | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
53	| Bulk import | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
54	| Site configuration | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
55	| User management | ✓ | — | — | — |
56	| Audit log + verify chain | ✓ | — | — | ✓ |
57	| Data quality reports | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ |
58	| Export/import packages | ✓ | — | — | — |
59	| Reconciliation status | ✓ | — | — | ✓ |
60	
61	### 3.4 Encryption Key Lifecycle
62	- Derived with PBKDF2 (100,000 iterations, SHA-256) from login password on login
63	- Held only in React auth context memory (`CryptoKey` object)
64	- Never serialized or written to disk
65	- Cleared on logout or 8-hour session expiry
66	- Required for read/write of `customerName`, `customerPlate`, `invoiceNotes`
67	
68	## 4. IndexedDB Schema (Dexie v1)
69	
70	### Hot Store (Active — last 90 days)
71	```
72	users              ++id, &username, role, siteId
73	sessions_auth      ++id, userId, createdAt, lastActiveAt
74	sites              ++id, &siteCode, name
75	bays               ++id, siteId, stationId, connectorId, status
76	reservations       ++id, bayId, siteId, userId, status, scheduledStart, &operationId
77	sessions_charging  ++id, reservationId, bayId, siteId, status, heartbeatAt, version
78	orders             ++id, sessionId, siteId, &orderNumber, status, version
79	notifications      ++id, recipientId, templateKey, status, isRead, createdAt, retries
80	auditLogs          ++id, actor, action, entityType, entityId, siteId, timestamp
81	importBatches      ++id, siteId, status, createdAt, dedupeHash
82	importRows         ++id, batchId, rowIndex, status
83	qualityReports     ++id, siteId, generatedAt, type
84	```
85	
86	### Cold Store (Archived — records > 90 days)
87	```
88	reservations_cold  ++id, bayId, siteId, originalId, archivedAt
89	sessions_cold      ++id, bayId, siteId, originalId, archivedAt
90	orders_cold        ++id, siteId, originalId, archivedAt
91	```
92	
93	## 5. Key Workflows
94	
95	### 5.1 Reservation → Check-In → Session → Order
96	```
97	Create Reservation (Scheduled, QR generated)
98	    → 10-min window passes without check-in → NoShow (auto-cancel)
99	    → Attendant checks in (manual or QR scan) → CheckedIn → Active session starts
100	        → Heartbeat updates every 30s
101	        → Temp leave (optional, within limits)
102	        → 30+ min no heartbeat → Anomaly flagged
103	        → Complete session → order auto-generated (Draft)
104	            → Submit → Paid (standard) or approval flow (compensation)
105	```
106	
107	### 5.2 Hot/Cold Tiering
108	```
109	App boot / every 24h:
110	  Records > 90 days old:
111	    hot.reservations → cold.reservations_cold
112	    hot.sessions_charging → cold.sessions_cold
113	    hot.orders → cold.orders_cold
114	```
115	
116	### 5.3 Bulk Import
117	```
118	Upload CSV/Excel
119	  → Deduplicate file (SHA-256 of content)
120	  → Validate all rows (collect all errors)
121	  → If ANY error → reject ALL, show error list, write zero records
122	  → If all valid → dedup rows, insert in single transaction
123	  → Notify actor: IMPORT_COMPLETE or IMPORT_FAILED
124	```
125	
126	## 6. Schedulers (App.tsx setInterval)
127	
128	| Interval | Function | Purpose |
129	|---|---|---|
130	| 30s | `heartbeatService.tick()` | Update heartbeat on all active sessions |
131	| 60s | `heartbeatService.checkAnomalies()` | Flag sessions missing heartbeat or over temp-leave limit |
132	| 60s | `reservationService.autoProcessNoShows()` | Cancel reservations past 10-min no-show deadline |
133	| 60s | `notificationService.retryFailed()` (via 30s) | Retry failed notifications |
134	| 60s | Due reminder / overdue alert checks | Fire DUE_REMINDER (15min before) and OVERDUE_ALERT (+5min) |
135	| 24h | `tieringService.runTiering()` | Move old records to cold store |
136	| Weekly (boot check) | `qualityService.runReport()` | Generate weekly data quality report |
137

1	# Questions & Ambiguities — TASK-121 ChargeBay
2	
3	## 1. Delivery / Project Type
4	- **Question**: The prompt says "runs fully standalone in the browser." Is there no backend?
5	- **My Understanding**: Correct — IndexedDB is the sole database. No NestJS, no network calls. The NestJS/PostgreSQL mention is "designed to be compatible" for a future migration, not a current requirement.
6	- **Solution**: Deliver as `pure_frontend` (Vite + React + TypeScript). `npm run dev` is the only start command.
7	
8	## 2. QR Code Generation Library
9	- **Question**: The prompt says "locally generated QR code" — should a QR library be used?
10	- **My Understanding**: A QR library is needed to generate scannable 2D barcodes. The `qrcode` package is pure JS with no network calls.
11	- **Solution**: Add `qrcode@^1` to package.json. Generate QR codes from `{ reservationId, operationId, siteCode }` JSON. Store as data URL in the reservation record.
12	
13	## 3. "Kiosk Mode" vs "Admin Mode" — Are These Separate Apps?
14	- **Question**: The prompt mentions "kiosk-mode and admin-mode navigation" — are these separate URL roots or a mode toggle?
15	- **My Understanding**: Two separate React Router layouts under `/kiosk/*` and `/admin/*`, selected automatically by role on login.
16	- **Solution**: Attendant and Site Manager land on `/kiosk`. Administrator and Auditor land on `/admin`. Both are in the same SPA bundle.
17	
18	## 4. "Site-Based Data Scope" — Multi-Site Support
19	- **Question**: Does each user belong to exactly one site, or can they manage multiple?
20	- **My Understanding**: SystemAdministrator has global access (all sites). All other roles are scoped to exactly one `siteId`. Accessing records from a different site throws a scope violation.
21	- **Solution**: `assertSiteScope(actor, recordSiteId)` helper enforced in every service method. `siteId` stored on the `User` record; null for SystemAdministrator.
22	
23	## 5. Heartbeat — Who Updates It?
24	- **Question**: The prompt mentions "active session heartbeat update." In a browser SPA with no server, who sends the heartbeat?
25	- **My Understanding**: The browser itself updates heartbeat via a `setInterval` scheduler while the app is open. If the app is closed, no heartbeat fires — this is expected offline behavior.
26	- **Solution**: `heartbeatService.tick()` runs every 30 seconds via `setInterval` in `App.tsx`, updating `heartbeatAt` on all active sessions for the current site.
27	
28	## 6. Excel/CSV Import — `.xlsx` Support
29	- **Question**: The prompt says "Excel/CSV bulk import." Does this require true `.xlsx` parsing?
30	- **My Understanding**: Yes — use the `xlsx` (SheetJS) package, which is pure JS with no network calls.
31	- **Solution**: Add `xlsx@^0.18` to `package.json`. Both `.csv` and `.xlsx` are accepted on the file picker.
32	
33	## 7. Compensation Approval — Who Requests vs Who Approves
34	- **Question**: Any role can request a compensation order, or only specific roles?
35	- **My Understanding**: Attendants and Site Managers can flag a compensation adjustment on an order. Approval requires SiteManager or SystemAdministrator.
36	- **Solution**: Any authenticated user can set `billingType = 'Compensation'` on an order they're creating. Approval flow locks the order at `Pending` until a SiteManager/Administrator approves.
37	
38	## 8. "Signed JSON Packages" — Signing Key
39	- **Question**: What is the signing key for the export package? A separate admin key, or the user's login password?
40	- **My Understanding**: A separately entered export password (not the login password) is used for both encryption and signature derivation, keeping the user's login credentials independent.
41	- **Solution**: Export/import modals prompt for a dedicated export password. Signature = SHA-256(JSON + exportPassword). AES-GCM key = PBKDF2(exportPassword + randomSalt).
42	
43	## 9. Rate Limiting — Window Definition
44	- **Question**: "200 rows per minute" — is this a sliding window or a fixed 1-minute bucket?
45	- **My Understanding**: A fixed 1-minute bucket is simpler and sufficient for the offline use case.
46	- **Solution**: `RateLimiter` uses a fixed window: store `{ count, windowStart }` in LocalStorage; reset when `now - windowStart > 60_000`.
47	
48	## 10. Auditor Role — Write Access
49	- **Question**: The prompt says Auditor has "read-only access to immutable logs and reports." Can Auditors do anything write?
50	- **My Understanding**: Auditors cannot create, update, or delete any business records. They can only view and export.
51	- **Solution**: All mutating service methods check `actor.role !== 'Auditor'` and throw `RBAC_AUDITOR_READ_ONLY` if violated.
52

1	# ChargeBay Offline Operations Console
2	
3	ChargeBay is a fully offline SPA used by EV charging site teams for reservations, check-in, sessions, orders, notifications, imports, data quality governance, and audit operations. The application runs entirely in the browser with no backend or HTTP dependencies, and persists state in IndexedDB (Dexie) plus small user/site preferences in LocalStorage.
4	
5	The architecture is service-first: UI pages/components call typed service modules, services enforce RBAC and security checks, and all business events are logged in a tamper-evident audit chain. Sensitive fields are encrypted with AES-GCM using keys derived at login time and kept in memory only.
6	
7	## Tech Stack
8	
9	| Layer | Technology |
10	|---|---|
11	| Framework | React 18 + TypeScript |
12	| Build | Vite 5 |
13	| Routing | React Router 6 |
14	| Local DB | Dexie.js 3 (IndexedDB hot/cold stores) |
15	| Crypto | WebCrypto (PBKDF2 + AES-GCM + SHA-256) |
16	| Import | CSV parser + xlsx |
17	| Testing | Vitest + React Testing Library + Playwright |
18	
19	## Quick Start
20	
21	```bash
22	npm install
23	npm run dev
24	```
25	
26	App URL: `http://localhost:5173`
27	
28	## Test Credentials
29	
30	| Username | Password | Role | Mode |
31	|---|---|---|---|
32	| `sysadmin` | `ChargeBay#Admin1` | SystemAdministrator | Admin |
33	| `manager` | `ChargeBay#Mgr01` | SiteManager | Kiosk |
34	| `attendant` | `ChargeBay#Att01` | Attendant | Kiosk |
35	| `auditor` | `ChargeBay#Aud01` | Auditor | Admin (read-only) |
36	
37	## Navigation Modes
38	
39	- **Kiosk mode**: Attendant and SiteManager (`/kiosk/...`)
40	- **Admin mode**: SystemAdministrator and Auditor (`/admin/...`)
41	
42	Route and UI access are role-gated, and site-scoped access is enforced in service-layer logic.
43	
44	## Roles
45	
46	- **SystemAdministrator**: Full global access to all features, configuration, users, import/export, archiving.
47	- **SiteManager**: Daily operations, pricing configuration, import for their assigned site.
48	- **Attendant**: Check-in, reservation creation, session management at their assigned site.
49	- **Auditor**: Read-only access to audit logs, quality reports, sessions/orders view, and notification send logs. Cannot perform any data mutations.
50	
51	## Session Restore & Re-Unlock
52	
53	The encryption key is derived from your password at login time and kept in memory only. If you refresh the page or your session is restored from localStorage, the app will show a **Re-Unlock** modal asking you to re-enter your password to derive the encryption key again. Until unlocked, pages that require encrypted data access will indicate that the key is not available.
54	
55	You can also choose to **Logout** from the re-unlock modal.
56	
57	## Theme
58	
59	A light/dark theme toggle is available in the shell header. Your preference is persisted in LocalStorage.
60	
61	## LocalStorage Settings
62	
63	The following settings are stored in LocalStorage (not IndexedDB):
64	- **Theme** (`cb_theme`): light or dark
65	- **Last selected site** (`cb_last_site`): used by SystemAdministrator for site-scoped operations
66	- **Notification preferences** (`cb_notif_prefs_{userId}`): per-user notification template toggles and desktop banner setting
67	- **Site configuration** (`cb_site_config_{siteId}`): operational parameters like rate, temp leave, no-show grace period
68	
69	## Build
70	
71	```bash
72	npm run build
73	```
74	
75	Output is written to `dist/`.
76	
77	## Preview
78	
79	```bash
80	npm run preview
81	```
82	
83	Serves the production build locally.
84	
85	## Run All Tests
86	
87	```bash
88	# Unit + Component tests (Vitest)
89	npm run test
90	
91	# E2E tests (Playwright, uses managed dev server)
92	npx playwright test
93	
94	# Full suite
95	bash run_tests.sh
96	```
97	
98	## Export/Import Packages
99	
100	Use **Admin Dashboard → Export & Archive** (SystemAdministrator only):
101	
102	1. Pick date range + export password, click **Export Package**.
103	2. Transfer generated JSON package to another device.
104	3. Select package file + password, click **Import Package**.
105	
106	Packages are AES-GCM encrypted and password-integrity-verified (`SHA-256(payload + password)`) before insertion.
107	> **Note:** The `signature` field is a password-bound integrity check, not an asymmetric cryptographic signature.
108	> It proves the package was created with the correct password and has not been tampered with, but does not establish signer identity.
109	
110	## Bulk Import
111	
112	Use **Bulk Import** page (SystemAdministrator, SiteManager):
113	
114	1. Select import type (reservations, orders, sessions).
115	2. Upload CSV or XLSX file.
116	3. Map fields, validate, then import.
117	
118	Imported sensitive fields (customer name, plate, invoice notes) are encrypted at rest when an encryption key is available.
119	
120	## Architecture Notes
121	
122	- **Service layer**: all mutation/query business logic in `src/services/*`
123	- **RBAC enforcement**: `assertCanMutate()` blocks Auditor from all data mutations at the service layer; `assertSiteScope()` enforces site-based row filtering
124	- **Hot/Cold tiering**: active tables + archived `_cold` tables; 90-day archival policy
125	- **Auth and key lifecycle**: PBKDF2 password verification, lockout handling, in-memory encryption key, key cleared on logout, re-unlock required after session restore
126	- **Audit chain**: append-only logs with chain hash verification
127	- **Encryption at rest**: customer name, customer plate, and invoice notes are encrypted via AES-GCM before storage in IndexedDB across all write paths (manual create, import, order generation)

The following settings are stored in LocalStorage (not IndexedDB):
- **Theme** (`cb_theme`): light or dark
- **Last selected site** (`cb_last_site`): used by SystemAdministrator for site-scoped operations
- **Notification preferences** (`cb_notif_prefs_{userId}`): per-user notification template toggles and desktop banner setting
- **Site configuration** (`cb_site_config_{siteId}`): operational parameters like rate, temp leave, no-show grace period", "new_string": "## Storage Layout

### IndexedDB (primary persistence)
All business data, audit logs, site configuration (`siteConfigs` table), and rate-limit state (`rateLimits` table) are persisted in IndexedDB via Dexie.js.

### LocalStorage (lightweight UI/session pointers only)
- **Theme** (`cb_theme`): light or dark
- **Last selected site** (`cb_last_site`): used by SystemAdministrator for site-scoped operations
- **Notification preferences** (`cb_notif_prefs_{userId}`): per-user notification template toggles and desktop banner setting
- **Site config cache** (`cb_site_config_{siteId}`): synced mirror of IndexedDB site config for synchronous reads"}
